__NOTOC__ {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Centre Game| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pd|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= | |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C21–C22]] |parent=[[Chess/Open Game|Open Game]] }} ==Centre Game== Black goes in the direction of the Centre Game by capturing the white e-pawn. === White's responses === The usual move is '''3. Qxd4'''. '''3. c3''' is the Danish Gambit. '''3. Nf3''', the Kieseritzky variation, often leads to several transpositions. Black can transpose to the [[Chess/Scotch Game|Scotch]] with 3...Nc6 or 3...Bb4+, to the [[Chess/Philidor Defence|Philidor]] with 3...d6, to [[Chess/Petrov's Defence|Petrov's]] Steinitz variation with 3...Nf6, or to the Lewis Gambit of the [[Chess/Bishop's Opening|Bishop's Opening]] with 3...Bc5 (followed by 4. Bc4 Nf6). An independent line is 3...Bc5 4. Nxd4 Nf6. ==Theory table== '''1. e4 e5 2. d4 exd4''' {| class="wikitable" |- !   !! 3 !! 4 !! Evaluation !! Notes |- !
